title: Subject Lines That Get Opened Quiz

questions.question: What percentage of recipients open an email based on the subject line alone?
questions.explanation: Nearly half of your audience decides whether to read your work based solely on the 3-5 words in your subject line.

questions.question: Which psychological state is a major driver of email opens?
questions.explanation: Emails that trigger curiosity (e.g., 'Quick thought about your API docs') are highly effective because the reader wants to know the context.

questions.question: Why should you avoid using ALL CAPS and multiple exclamation marks in subject lines?

options.text: It makes the subject too long
It is difficult for older screens to display
It creates 'Sales Stench' and is a major trigger for spam filters
It is considered rude in some countries

questions.explanation: Spam filters (and human brains) associate aggressive formatting with junk mail. To get into the inbox, you must sound like a professional individual, not a marketing bot.

questions.question: What is the recommended length for a cold email subject line?

options.text: As long as possible to include all the benefits
Short enough to fit in a 30-character mobile preview (usually 3-6 words)

questions.explanation: Since most people check email on mobile, keeping it short ensures the entire subject is visible in the notification and preview window.

questions.question: What is 'A/B Testing' in the context of subject lines?

options.text: Writing two different subject lines for the same campaign to see which one gets a higher open rate
Sending emails to both Gmail and Yahoo users

questions.explanation: A/B testing allows you to use data instead of guesswork to optimize your results over time.

questions.question: Choose your #1 Pillar. Write two different subject lines for a campaign targeting your core ICP. One should be 'Personalized' and one should be a 'Question.' Which do you think will perform better and why?
questions.promptForAI: Evaluate the quality of the subject lines. They should avoid spam words and be concise. The justification should show an understanding of their specific audience's behavior.
